content.body:
Hi Simon Zünd

I finally resumed work on Scopes implementation on Terser, and I'm trying to test if my generated map is working properly on Chrome dev tools. Last Thursday I figured out that I had some issues because I was missing the update of `chrome-devtools__source-map-scopes-codec` to `v0.8.0`. After updating it, this looked like it was working, but now I'm trying a simple example, but I can't make a source map that is properly loading scopes information for Chrome Dev Tools. I've tried on both Chrome Canary and on Chrome `Version 146.0.7680.165`, but the scopes are not loading there.

Here is the source[1], its minified version[2] and the generated SourceMap with scopes[3]. To do some debugging, I'm using a tool to decode scopes using the same codec from dev tools[4], and the output I'm getting for this current map is the following[5].

I didn't look deep into Chrome Dev tools code yet, but I'm wondering if there's something too obvious that I'm missing here. I tested with the flag "use scope information from source maps" enabled and disabled. What I'm getting as the result is the stack from the image above, missing the local `z` with its value, and it should be there, since the source map is including such variable into the scope.

[1] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-example-js
[2] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-example-min-js
[3] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-example-min-js-map
[4] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-decode-scopes-mjs

[5] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-decoded_scopes-json
content.formatted_body:
Hi Simon Zünd

I finally resumed work on Scopes implementation on Terser, and I'm trying to test if my generated map is working properly on Chrome dev tools. Last Thursday I figured out that I had some issues because I was missing the update of chrome-devtools__source-map-scopes-codec to v0.8.0. After updating it, this looked like it was working, but now I'm trying a simple example, but I can't make a source map that is properly loading scopes information for Chrome Dev Tools. I've tried on both Chrome Canary and on Chrome Version 146.0.7680.165, but the scopes are not loading there.

Here is the source[1], its minified version[2] and the generated SourceMap with scopes[3]. To do some debugging, I'm using a tool to decode scopes using the same codec from dev tools[4], and the output I'm getting for this current map is the following[5].

I didn't look deep into Chrome Dev tools code yet, but I'm wondering if there's something too obvious that I'm missing here. I tested with the flag "use scope information from source maps" enabled and disabled. What I'm getting as the result is the stack from the image above, missing the local z with its value, and it should be there, since the source map is including such variable into the scope.

[1] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-example-js
[2] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-example-min-js
[3] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-example-min-js-map
[4] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-decode-scopes-mjs

[5] - https://gist.github.com/caiolima/ae96edfb7d44f7b53f69a0d252776bf8#file-decoded_scopes-json
